last_item: Band-1.5: wire scheduled backups (full + incremental) into the runtime + process-local single-flight guard so overlaps don't false-alarm
last_result: done â€” a left-alone box now backs itself up. The scheduler was instantiated but only ran the telemetry/alert loop, so SCHEDULED BACKUPS HAD NEVER RUN (only POST /backups/run did). startBackgroundJobs now registers 'full-backup' + 'incremental-backup' cron jobs from cfg.Schedules (defaults: weekly full Sun 03:00, daily incremental Mon-Sat 03:00) via registerBackupJob. Each scheduledBackup(type) self-heals the pgBackRest config (config.Loadâ†’ensureBackupConfigured â€” same prereq the manual button runs; a panel that started before PG was reachable never wrote it) then calls backups.Backup. Empty spec = operator opt-out â†’ job NOT registered + loud Warn. Closed the overlap gap the prior note flagged: the Owner guard is cross-PANEL only (repo markers); it does NOT stop two concurrent Backup() calls in one process â€” they'd collide on pgBackRest's own on-disk lock and the loser would be recorded as a 'fail' row â†’ false critical backup-failed alert. Added Manager.backupMu (sync.Mutex via TryLock): an overlap (manual during scheduled, or a long full overlapping the next incremental) returns a typed CodeConflict SKIP with NO history row; the scheduled job swallows CodeConflict (Info log, returns nil) so the scheduler never logs it as a failure. No reentrancy: Restoreâ†’takeSafetyBackupâ†’Backup does not hold backupMu (nested safety backup acquires it cleanly; a restore launched during an active backup safely HARD-STOPs since it can't take its pre-restore safety backup). Tests: manager concurrent-skip (no fail row, pgBackRest never invoked); server jobs-registered + empty-schedule-disabled. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no blocking issues. Full Go gate green (gofmt/vet/test/build); web untouched.
notes: Band 1.5 (data durability) IN PROGRESS. Done: (1) 'last good backup was N ago' surfaced (Dashboard + Backups banner). (2) backup-failed/backup-stale rules + metrics + Collector.enrichBackup. (3) telemetry+alert evaluation LOOP wired (background.go) â€” alerts fire in prod. (4) ***SCHEDULED BACKUPS NOW RUN: full+incremental cron jobs registered in startBackgroundJobs; Manager.backupMu single-flight guard makes overlaps a CodeConflict skip (no fail row, no false alert).*** Remaining 1.5: nudge backups off-host (S3 default, warn local-only â€” next top 1.5 item); restore-test SURFACING + EXECUTION (handleRestoreTest is still a stub returning typed 'not implemented' â€” wiring its execution is separate from this); optionally wire OTLP exporter (nil in the collector). Plumbing: alert engine reads Snapshot FIELDS via alert/metrics.go metricValue (separate key namespace from telemetry/snapshot.go MetricKeys). Backups: internal/backup/manager.go Backup() takes backupMu.TryLock first, then ownership, writes terminal 'fail'/'success'; internal/store/backup.go (ListBackups newest-first, LatestSuccessfulBackup). Scheduler: internal/scheduler â€” empty spec disables (Register no-op); Jobs() lists registered. go/psql/pg_ctl snap-sandboxed (run sandbox-disabled). TMPDIR empty in some shells â€” use /tmp.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
